title: calonyction bona-nox var. normale
intro: Calonyction bona-nox var. normale, also known as the "devil's claw," is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Convolvulaceae. It is a perennial vine native to southern Africa and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Calonyction bona-nox var. normale include relieving pain and inflammation, reducing stress and anxiety, and aiding in the management of arthritis and other musculoskeletal conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, capsules, or tinctures, and are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and restlessness.
